$comment: The done-contract IS the product spec. It is authored and adversarially reviewed (challenged for vagueness, unfalsifiable checks, and missing acceptance items) BEFORE the build/verify loop runs. Each item's check must be objective and machine-checkable so the loop can decide pass/fail without re-litigating intent. A Workflow script consumes these field names verbatim, so do not rename them.
title: Goalkeeper Done-Contract

goal.description: One sentence stating what "finished" means for this task. This is the headline the acceptance items collectively prove.

items.description: Acceptance items. Each is independently checkable and contributes one objective pass/fail signal to the loop.

id.description: Short kebab-case identifier, unique within the contract (e.g. "middleware-exists").

priority.description: Optional execution priority. LOWER = higher priority; 1 is done first. Within the set of items whose dependencies are all satisfied, the loop runs the lowest-priority number first. Defaults to a large value when omitted, so explicitly-prioritized items go first.

description.description: What to build or verify for this item, in plain language.

expectedOutput.description: Optional. The concrete artifact or observable result that proves this item is done (e.g. "a 429 response with a Retry-After header on the (N+1)th request"). Distinct from `check`: the check is how the loop measures pass/fail; expectedOutput is what the builder is aiming to produce.

dependsOn.description: Optional. Item ids that MUST pass before this item is eligible to run. Enables dependency-ordered scheduling: the loop only picks an item once every id listed here is already passing. Use [] or omit for an independent item. A dependsOn id that is not present in the contract triggers a dependency-deadlock escalation.

check.description: The objective, machine-checkable acceptance test. A base payload is selected by `type`: type=command -> `command`; type=file_exists -> `path`; type=grep -> `pattern` (matched against the file/output at `path`); type=judge -> `rubric`; type=pipeline -> `build`+`verify` (optional `deploy`). Any check may additionally carry the hardening modifiers below: `nondeterministic`/`passPolicy`/`precondition` (flaky/hardware checks), `shell`/`cwd`/`env` (run under a specific shell), and `humanPrecondition`/`humanRearm` (a physical step a person must perform first).

type.description: Which kind of check this is. Determines which payload field(s) are required.

command.description: For type=command: a shell command. Exit code 0 = pass, non-zero = fail.

path.description: For type=file_exists: a path that must exist. Also used by type=grep as the file to match `pattern` against.

pattern.description: For type=grep: a regular expression that must match (typically within the file at `path`).

rubric.description: For type=judge: criteria an LLM judge applies to score the item pass or fail. Reserve for checks that genuinely cannot be expressed as a deterministic command.

build.description: For type=pipeline: the build command, run first. A non-zero exit FAILS the check immediately and deploy/verify do not run (never deploy a failed build).

deploy.description: For type=pipeline: an optional deploy/flash command, run after a clean build and before verify.

verify.description: For type=pipeline: the command that decides pass/fail, run after build (and deploy). The pipeline passes only if this exits 0.

freshBuild.description: For type=pipeline: force a clean/reconfigured build so a stale incremental artifact cannot masquerade as a passing deploy.

nondeterministic.description: Declare a check whose pass can depend on timing, hardware, a peer device, or the network. The verifier then retries per `passPolicy` and latches the git tree-hash on success, so a later flaky miss at the same code state is not read as a regression. An environment-dependent check MUST set this (the spec-review treats its absence as a blocking gap).

passPolicy.description: For a `nondeterministic` check: how many of how many retries must pass.

mode.description: latch (default): pass if it succeeds at least once across n attempts. k-of-n: pass if it succeeds at least k of n times.

precondition.description: An optional command run before each attempt of a `nondeterministic` check (e.g. arm the radio, reset a fixture).

shell.description: Pin the shell the check command runs under (instead of the runner's default). On Windows, pwsh = PowerShell (never MSYS), cmd = cmd.exe.

cwd.description: Working directory for the check command, relative to the repo root (default: repo root).

env.description: Environment variables set for the check command.

humanPrecondition.description: A real-world action a PERSON must perform before this check can pass (e.g. 'move Unit B 50m away and start the listener'). Requires humanGate (or approveToken) enabled: the run suspends at zero compute (status awaiting-human) until the person confirms via resolution.json {token, action:"ready"}, then resumes and verifies. With the gate off, the run escalates (human-gate-disabled).

humanRearm.description: When a satisfied humanPrecondition re-arms (asks again): per-tree (default: only when the baseline tree changes), per-run (once per fresh invocation), once (never re-ask after the first confirmation).

allowTestEdit.description: Whether the builder may modify the files this check depends on. Default false. When false, the builder must satisfy the check as written and may NOT edit the check's files to make it pass (no moving the goalposts).

candidates.description: Optional per-item best-of-N override (opt-in). When > 1 (or the global caps.candidates is > 1), this item is built by N diverse candidate solutions run sequentially on the live repo, and the deterministic verifier picks the winner. Default: inherit caps.candidates (1 = single builder).
